description: TAF's 5 foundational axioms extracted as structured data for fieldlink consumption. These axioms ground Rosetta's merge policy — any family-shape merge must satisfy all 5.

axioms.name: Conservation of Energy
axioms.statement: No organism can output more than it takes in. Burnout and breakdown are the same event at different substrates.
axioms.formal: E_out ≤ E_in for any system over any time interval

axioms.name: Entropy Increases
axioms.statement: Every process generates waste. The question is whether waste is managed or whether it accumulates until the system fails.
axioms.violation_signature: entropy decrease in isolated system, or waste accumulation without management pathway

axioms.name: Prediction Error Minimization
axioms.statement: Organisms expend energy maintaining predictive models. Turbulent environments inflate prediction costs and diminish net efficiency.
axioms.formal: E_prediction = f(model_complexity, environment_turbulence). Net_efficiency = E_productive / (E_productive + E_prediction)
axioms.violation_signature: prediction cost exceeds energy budget, or perfect prediction claimed from noisy data

axioms.name: Organism Agnosticism
axioms.statement: Framework applies identically to biological and mechanical systems. If thermodynamics don't work, the substrate is irrelevant.
axioms.formal: For any law L and substrates S1, S2: L(S1) = L(S2)
axioms.violation_signature: claiming a physical law applies to one substrate but not another

axioms.name: Measurement Over Narrative
axioms.statement: Cultural labels stop investigation. Energy accounting continues until it finds actual causes. The two approaches are incompatible.
axioms.formal: confidence(measurement) > confidence(narrative) for any physical claim
axioms.violation_signature: cultural explanation accepted without energy accounting, or measurement replaced by narrative
